summary: The contract proposal is comprehensive, well-scoped to F1 (turn-based grid world & 8-directional movement), and faithful to Sprint 1's stated theme (project scaffold, entity/tile/grid types, ASCII render loop, walls). Criteria are concrete, individually testable (each cites observable Python-level behavior, exit codes, character counts, or position equalities), and the out-of-scope list correctly defers procedural generation, doors-on-map, stairs traversal, FOV, combat, items, monsters, save/load, and the Whisperer to later sprints. The 21 criteria cover all eight stated deliverables with no gaps I can find.

F1 - Turn-based grid world & 8-dir movem.notes: Tile model (C4), grid (C5), Entity/Player (C6), Game container (C7), 8-directional movement (C11), wall collision (C12), out-of-bounds collision (C13), and turn counter (C14) all explicitly covered. Bump-to-attack and FOV from F1's full spec are intentionally deferred to Sprint 3, which matches the sprint plan.

coverageAssessment.deliverableCoverage: All 10 deliverables map to at least one criterion. Package+__main__: C1/C2. Module separation: C3. Tile abstraction: C4. Floor/grid: C5. Player+movement: C6/C11/C12/C13. Renderer: C8/C9/C10. CLI flags: C15/C16. Test suite: C17/C18. README: C19. (Commit convention is a process expectation, not a runtime criterion -- correctly omitted from automated criteria.)

strengths: Each criterion has explicit testSteps with concrete assertions (e.g. 'frame_rows[py][px] == @', 'exactly 5 lines and each line exactly 10 chars wide', 'count of @ characters is exactly 1'). No vague 'works correctly' language.
C16 (--seed reproducibility) is correctly weakened to byte-identity-of-same-seed rather than seed-1-vs-seed-2-differ, which would be untestable when Sprint 1 may use a single static floor.
C13 explicitly tests that out-of-bounds is a no-op rather than an exception -- catches a common bug class.
C10 enumerates the legal glyph set as a closed character set, which makes 'no emoji / no ANSI noise' actually verifiable.
C20 + C21 add hygiene gates (no third-party deps, no stderr noise) that catch sloppy scaffolds.
Assumptions section explicitly permits a stdout frame renderer instead of a real TUI, removing a common ambiguity that would otherwise force a curses dependency.
Out-of-scope list is detailed and references the specific later sprint that will own each deferred feature.

concerns.issue: C7 requires Game to be 'constructible from a seed' but does not require the seed to actually influence anything in Sprint 1. Combined with C16's permissive language, an implementation could accept --seed and ignore it. This is acceptable for Sprint 1 (single static floor) but the generator should be aware that Sprint 2 will tighten this.
concerns.suggestedFix: None required. Document in Sprint 2's contract that --seed must actually drive generation.

concerns.issue: C14 says 'Issue at least one valid move and one wall-bump. Verify the counter advanced by at least 1 after each action.' A wall-bump is ambiguous in many roguelikes -- some count it as a turn, some don't. The criterion accepts either policy ('exact increment policy is up to the implementer'), which is fine, but the test step should make clear that the wall-bump increment is optional or be split.
concerns.suggestedFix: Optional clarification: 'Verify the counter advanced after the valid move; the wall-bump policy is implementer's choice but must be consistent.' Not required for acceptance.

concerns.issue: C2 accepts either '--help' OR a '--headless/--no-tty' equivalent for the clean-exit check. If the implementer chooses --help and argparse prints to stdout with exit 0, that's fine; but a hand-rolled CLI that exits 2 on --help would technically fail. Acceptable as written since C15 separately exercises --headless.
regressionSprints is empty, which is correct for Sprint 1 (no prior sprints).

testabilityCheck.notes: Every criterion specifies (a) a testMethod (static/unit/integration), (b) ordered testSteps, and (c) a threshold. Thresholds are observable booleans (exit code, character equality, count, presence-of-substring), not subjective judgments.

completenessCheck.notes: No Sprint 2+ behavior leaks into Sprint 1's criteria. Door/stairs glyphs are allowed in the legal-character set (C10) but not required to appear, which correctly matches the assumption that those tile kinds may be defined in the data model without being placed.

recommendation: Accept as-is. The proposal is more rigorous than typical Sprint 1 contracts and gives both the generator and evaluator unambiguous targets. The minor concerns above are observations, not blockers.
